description: Improve three areas of the side panel UX: (1) reduce visual monotony in long tool lists by collapsing disabled tools and adding subtle visual differentiation for active tools, (2) enrich the search experience with better no-results messaging and visual hierarchy, and (3) elevate the disconnected/empty states to match the retro design language with character and actionable guidance.

userStories.title: Collapse off tools under a toggle to reduce tool list density
userStories.description: When a plugin has many tools and some are set to 'off', visually collapse the off tools behind a 'Show N hidden' toggle so the expanded tool list is shorter and the enabled tools are more prominent.
acceptanceCriteria: When a plugin's expanded tool list has 3 or more tools with permission 'off', the off tools are collapsed behind a toggle row
The toggle row shows 'N hidden' and clicking it reveals/hides the off tools
Tools with permission 'ask' or 'auto' are always visible (never collapsed)
When all tools are enabled (none off), no toggle is shown
When there are fewer than 3 off tools, they are shown normally (no toggle)
The toggle remembers its state within the current session (does not reset when accordion closes/opens)
userStories.notes: Files: - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx (add hidden tools toggle logic) - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/BrowserToolsCard.tsx (same treatment) - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.stories.tsx (add story for collapsed tools) - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/BrowserToolsCard.stories.tsx (add story) Context: Currently when you expand a plugin with 30+ tools (like browser tools), every single tool row is displayed regardless of permission state. The result is a long flat wall of identical-looking rows. Tools set to 'off' take up visual space without providing value — the user already decided they don't want them. The tool list rendering happens in two places: 1. PluginCard.tsx lines 234-269 — renders `toolGroups` (grouped) or flat `visibleTools` 2. BrowserToolsCard.tsx lines 159-192 — identical pattern Both use the `ToolRow` component for each tool. Design: Within each group (or the flat list), separate tools into two buckets: - `enabledTools`: permission !== 'off' - `hiddenTools`: permission === 'off' Render enabled tools normally. If `hiddenTools.length >= 3`, render a toggle row at the bottom of the group: ```tsx ``` When `showHidden` is true, render the hidden tools below the toggle. State management: - Use `useState>` at the PluginCard/BrowserToolsCard level to track which groups have hidden tools expanded. The key is the group name (or 'flat' for ungrouped). - This state is ephemeral (resets on unmount) — no need to persist to storage. Threshold: - Only collapse when there are 3 or more off tools. With 1-2 off tools, the toggle button would save almost no space and add unnecessary interaction. Constraints: - Do NOT change ToolRow.tsx — the row component stays the same - Do NOT change tool-groups.ts — grouping logic stays the same - The toggle row must visually fit within the tool list — same horizontal padding (px-3), same border treatment (border-b border-border) - Import ChevronRight from lucide-react for the toggle chevron - Both PluginCard and BrowserToolsCard MUST have identical behavior

userStories.title: Add visual emphasis to active (in-use) tool rows
userStories.description: Make tool rows that are currently being executed by an AI agent visually distinct from idle tool rows, so the user can instantly see which tools are active.
acceptanceCriteria: Active tool rows have a visually distinct left-edge accent (e.g., primary-colored left border) that is NOT present on idle tool rows
The active state is clearly visible without requiring the user to look at the ToolIcon animation
The active emphasis does not cause layout shift or change row height
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ToolRow.tsx (add active row styling)
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ToolRow.stories.tsx (update Active story)

Context:
Currently, the only visual indicator that a tool is active is the ToolIcon's border animation (activity-border-flash). The rest of the row looks identical to idle rows. When you have 20+ tools visible, it's hard to spot which one is active.

The ToolRow component (ToolRow.tsx line 84-112) already receives an `active` prop. Currently it only passes it to `<ToolIcon>`.

Design:
Add a subtle left border accent to active rows, similar to how group headers use `border-l-2 border-l-primary`. The active row gets:
```
border-l-2 border-l-primary
```

And a subtle background tint:
```
bg-primary/5
```

This creates a clear visual rail: the yellow left edge + faint yellow tint makes the active row pop out of the list instantly. It complements the ToolIcon animation rather than replacing it.

Implementation:
In ToolRow.tsx, the root div's className currently uses:
```tsx
cn(
  'flex items-center gap-2 border-border border-b px-3 py-1.5 transition-colors last:border-b-0 even:bg-muted/10',
  enabled ? 'hover:bg-primary/10' : 'hover:bg-muted/50',
  disabled && 'opacity-50',
)
```

Add active state styling:
```tsx
cn(
  'flex items-center gap-2 border-border border-b px-3 py-1.5 transition-colors last:border-b-0 even:bg-muted/10',
  active && 'border-l-2 border-l-primary bg-primary/5',
  enabled ? 'hover:bg-primary/10' : 'hover:bg-muted/50',
  disabled && 'opacity-50',
)
```

Note: Adding `border-l-2` to a row that doesn't normally have it will shift content 2px right. To prevent layout shift, add `border-l-2 border-l-transparent` to ALL rows as a baseline, and only change the color for active rows:
```tsx
cn(
  'flex items-center gap-2 border-l-2 border-l-transparent border-border border-b px-3 py-1.5 transition-colors last:border-b-0 even:bg-muted/10',
  active && 'border-l-primary bg-primary/5',
  enabled ? 'hover:bg-primary/10' : 'hover:bg-muted/50',
  disabled && 'opacity-50',
)
```

This way all rows have a 2px left border (transparent by default, yellow when active) — zero layout shift.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the ToolIcon component
- Do NOT change the row height or padding
- The active emphasis must work in both light and dark mode
- The `border-l-primary` color must come from the theme (it does — it resolves to --primary)

userStories.title: Improve search no-results message with helpful suggestions
userStories.description: Replace the bare 'No results' text with a more helpful message that guides users toward productive next steps, following the UI skill's recommendation to never show dead-end empty states.
acceptanceCriteria: The no-results state shows the search query back to the user so they know what was searched
The message includes at least one actionable suggestion (e.g., 'Try a different keyword' or 'Search by plugin name or tool description')
The npm search error state shows more context than just 'Search failed'
The visual treatment uses the retro design language (font-mono, theme colors)
userStories.notes:
Files: - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/SearchResults.tsx (improve no-results and error states) - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/SearchResults.stories.tsx (update/add stories) Context: The current no-results message (SearchResults.tsx line 148): ```tsx {showNoResults &&
No results
} ``` The current npm search error (line 136): ```tsx
Search failed
``` Both are minimal plain text with no design personality. The UI skill says (Search > No Results, severity: Medium): 'Show no results with suggestions. Don't: Blank screen or 0 results.' And (Feedback > Empty States, severity: Medium): 'Show helpful message and action. Don't: Blank empty screens.' Design: 1. No results state — replace plain text with the retro Empty compound component: ```tsx {showNoResults && ( No results for “{toolFilter}” Try searching by plugin name, tool name, or description. )} ``` This uses the existing `retro/Empty` component which already has the correct card styling (border-2, rounded, shadow-md, bg-card). The Ghost icon default adds personality. 2. npm search error — make it more informative: ```tsx

Search unavailable

Could not reach the npm registry. Check your internet connection.

``` 3. Also improve the inline 'No tools matching X' message in PluginList.tsx (line 198-200): ```tsx return (
No tools matching “{toolFilter}”
); ``` Change to the same Empty component pattern for consistency. Constraints: - Import `Empty` from `./retro/Empty.js` in SearchResults.tsx (it's not currently imported) - Do NOT change the search input behavior or debounce timing - Do NOT change the npm search API call - The Empty component is already available — do not create a new component

userStories.title: Elevate disconnected and empty states with retro design personality
userStories.description: Improve the visual treatment of the disconnected states (connection refused, auth failed) and the 'no plugins installed' empty state to better leverage the retro design language and provide clearer, more helpful guidance.
acceptanceCriteria: The ConnectionRefusedState uses a visually distinct icon or illustration that conveys 'disconnected' in a retro style
The AuthFailedState uses a visually distinct icon that conveys 'locked/denied'
The 'No plugins installed' state in App.tsx uses the retro Empty component with a clear call to action
All empty states have role='status' or appropriate ARIA attributes for accessibility
The code block in ConnectionRefusedState has a copy-to-clipboard button
userStories.notes:
Files: - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/EmptyStates.tsx (improve all states) - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/EmptyStates.stories.tsx (add/update stories) - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx (improve 'no plugins' state at line 481-486) Context: 1. ConnectionRefusedState (EmptyStates.tsx lines 7-52): Currently uses the Empty compound component with title, separator, description, code block, and a Loader. This is functional but could be improved: - No icon — the Ghost default from Empty.Icon is not used. Adding a relevant icon (like `WifiOff` or `Unplug` from lucide-react) would give instant visual recognition. - The code block for the `opentabs start` command has no copy button — the user has to manually select and copy. - The 'Reconnecting...' loader is good. 2. AuthFailedState (EmptyStates.tsx lines 54-67): Same structure but no icon and no reconnection indicator. Adding a `ShieldX` or `KeyRound` icon would convey 'auth failed' instantly. 3. 'No plugins installed' (App.tsx lines 481-486): ```tsx {plugins.length === 0 && failedPlugins.length === 0 && (

No plugins installed

Search to discover and install plugins

)} ``` This is plain text — it should use the Empty component to match the design system and provide a stronger visual anchor. Design: 1. ConnectionRefusedState — add icon and copy button: ```tsx Cannot Reach MCP Server Start the MCP server:
{command}
Reconnecting...
``` For the copy button, add a brief visual feedback state (icon changes to `Check` for 1.5s after click) using useState. 2. AuthFailedState — add icon: ```tsx ``` Also add a copy button for the `chrome://extensions/` text. 3. 'No plugins installed' — use Empty component: ```tsx {plugins.length === 0 && failedPlugins.length === 0 && ( No Plugins Installed Use the search bar above to discover and install plugins from npm. )} ``` Import `Package` from lucide-react. Icons chosen: - `Unplug` for connection refused — visually says 'disconnected cable' - `KeyRound` for auth failed — visually says 'wrong key' - `Package` for no plugins — visually says 'empty package/box' All are available in lucide-react which is already a dependency. Constraints: - Do NOT change the ConnectionRefusedState's port-reading logic or the reconnection behavior - Do NOT change the chrome.storage mock in Storybook — the EmptyStates already work in Storybook - The copy button must use `navigator.clipboard.writeText()` — it works in the extension context - Icons must come from lucide-react (already installed) - Every empty state container should have the correct ARIA: Empty component already has appropriate semantics, but add `role='status'` to the reconnecting indicator - The Empty component uses `hover:shadow-none` by default — this is fine for disconnected states
